records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Hardened (-'nd); p. pr. & vb. n. Hardening (-'n*ng).]

defs: To make hard or harder; to make firm or compact; to indurate; as, to harden clay or iron.
To accustom by labor or suffering to endure with constancy; to strengthen; to stiffen; to inure; also, to confirm in wickedness or shame; to make unimpressionable.
quotes: I would harden myself in sorrow.

defs: To become hard or harder; to acquire solidity, or more compactness; as, mortar hardens by drying.
quotes: The deliberate judgment of those who knew him [A. Lincoln] has hardened into tradition.

defs: To become confirmed or strengthened, in either a good or a bad sense.
quotes: They, hardened more by what might most reclaim.
